sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Illinois Municipal Retirement Fund Has $3.15 Million Stock Position in Targa Resources, Inc. $TRGP
feed.summary: The Illinois Municipal Retirement Fund (IMRF) recently decreased its stake in Targa Resources (TRGP) by 19.3% in Q3, selling 4,515 shares and ending with a $3.15 million position. Targa Resources, a midstream energy company, has a market cap of $48.03 billion and a P/E ratio of 29.75, and recently paid a quarterly dividend of $1.00.

feed.title: BBVA Announces New Share Buyback Program
feed.summary: BBVA (Banco Bilbao Vizcaya Argentaria) has announced a new share buyback program to repurchase its own shares, signaling capital strength and supporting its capital management strategy. This move is expected to boost earnings per share, optimize its balance sheet, and enhance investor confidence in the bank's financial health. BBVA is a global financial institution focusing on retail and commercial banking, corporate and investment banking, and asset management in Europe and Latin America.

feed.title: Verizon Communications Inc. (NYSE:VZ) Stock Rockets 26% But Many Are Still Ignoring The Company
feed.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. (NYSE:VZ) shares have surged 26% within the last month and 20% over the past year, yet its P/E ratio of 12x remains low compared to the broader US market. This low P/E is attributed to the company's recent declining earnings. Despite analyst forecasts predicting a 12% annual earnings growth for the next three years, matching market averages, investors appear unconvinced, suggesting potential underlying risks or doubts about achieving these expectations.

feed.title: North America Dominates Medical Waste Management Market with 33% Revenue Share, Driving Expansion to USD 59.45 billion by 2030 | Key Players - Stericycle Inc., Sharp Compliance Inc., Waste Management Inc.
feed.summary: The global Medical Waste Management Market was valued at USD 34.08 billion in 2023 and is projected to grow to USD 59.45 billion by 2030, with a CAGR of 8.0%. North America currently leads with a 33% revenue share, largely due to stringent regulations and advanced healthcare infrastructure. The market's growth is fueled by expanding healthcare services, technological advancements in waste treatment, and increasing emphasis on eco-friendly practices, though high operational costs and inconsistent regulations pose challenges.

feed.title: After Earnings, Is Alphabet Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
feed.summary: Alphabet reported strong fiscal fourth-quarter earnings with sales up 18%, driven significantly by Google Cloud's 48% growth, affirming its position as an AI leader. Morningstar maintains a $340 fair value estimate, viewing the stock as fairly valued despite significant planned capital expenditures for AI in 2026. The company benefits from a wide economic moat and strong financial health but faces uncertainty from antitrust regulation and the high costs associated with AI investments.

feed.title: Mohawk Industries posts solid Q4 despite annual sales dip
feed.summary: Mohawk Industries reported a 2.4% increase in fourth-quarter 2025 revenue to $2.7 billion, despite a slight 0.5% dip in net sales for the full year, reaching $10.8 billion. The company achieved $90 million in Q4 net earnings and $370 million for the full year, driven by productivity gains and restructuring efforts. CEO Jeff Lorberbaum noted stable commercial demand but highlighted challenges from weak U.S. housing turnover, with the company focusing on profitable growth and market position ahead of a housing recovery.

feed.title: CRH Expands $300 Million Buyback With Fresh NYSE Share Repurchase
feed.summary: CRH plc has continued its share buyback program in the U.S., repurchasing 29,600 NYSE-listed shares at an average price of $124.93, which will be canceled. This action is part of a larger plan to repurchase up to $300 million in shares by February 2026, aiming to reduce the number of shares in issue and potentially boost earnings per share. The building materials company’s ongoing capital return strategy reflects management's confidence in its financial health and future prospects.

feed.title: Inside Coca‑Cola’s 2026 growth playbook: Digital, innovation and change
feed.summary: Coca-Cola is implementing a bold growth strategy for 2026 focused on digital transformation, innovation, and leadership changes. This includes appointing a new CEO, Henrique Braun, and a chief digital officer, Sedef Salingan Sahin, to drive digital prowess and efficiency. The company is also prioritizing "bigger and bolder innovation" in its product portfolio and localizing marketing strategies to recruit new consumers, while facing challenges like the future of Costa Coffee.

feed.title: Costco launches mobile app ordering for custom cakes and deli trays, easing customer woes
feed.summary: Costco is overhauling its custom cake and deli tray ordering system by introducing mobile app and website options, retiring a decades-old paper form process that customers found inconvenient. This digital move, which executives described as streamlining a "clunky" system, will allow shoppers to customize orders online. The feature is expected to be fully implemented across all locations by the end of 2026, making it easier for members, especially those living far from stores, to place orders.
